module.name: cli
module.spec: Run dispatches on args[0] to init, todo, reset, link, unlink, list, help, or skill. No args, --help, -h, and help all print the help text with exit code 0. Unknown commands return an error message with help text and exit code 1. Missing args for reset/link/unlink return a usage message with an example and exit code 1. For link, reset, and unlink, if args[1] is --help or -h, print command-specific usage with exit code 0. Reset accepts 1 arg (orphan cleanup) or 2 args (edge resolution).

The help command prints a multi-line reference: tool description, a prominent "First time? Run drift skill" suggestion, all commands with syntax, marker syntax (D! id=markerid), spec file format (main or module root, spec as direct child, module.spec qualification), quick start, and worked examples. Exit code 0.

The skill command prints a comprehensive embedded guide covering: what driftpin is, the full workflow (init, specs, markers, link, todo, reset), spec file format with module/import system, marker syntax and hash window, CLI command table, drift detection model, drift.pin and drift.ignore files. Exit code 0.

The init command creates drift.pin (empty state), and if main.pin.xml does not already exist, writes a starter template with a single example spec. The output message instructs the user to edit main.pin.xml, place markers, and run drift skill.

formatTodo distinguishes empty vs clean: if no specs and no markers exist, it returns "Nothing to check: no specs or markers registered." with guidance. If synced, it returns "No changes detected. N specs, M markers, K links in sync." Otherwise it prints counts of changed markers and specs, then lists each todo with edge description, file locations, drift reason (including deletion-specific message when a spec or marker has been deleted), and the reset command to run.

The reset command accepts either one or two arguments. With two arguments (marker and module.spec), it resolves a drifted edge. With one argument (an ID containing a dot for specs, no dot for markers), it removes an orphaned deleted entry from drift.pin. Missing arguments return a usage error showing both forms. On success with two args, returns empty string with exit code 0. On success with one arg, returns "Removed deleted spec/marker X from drift.pin". On error returns the error message with exit code 1.

The link command requires a marker and a module.spec argument, space-separated. Missing arguments return a usage error with an example. On success it returns "Linked marker X to spec Y" with exit code 0. On error it returns a prescriptive error message with exit code 1.

The unlink command requires a marker and a module.spec argument, space-separated. Missing arguments return a usage error with an example. On success it returns "Unlinked marker X from spec Y" with exit code 0. On error it returns the error message with exit code 1.

The list command shows all specs, markers, links, and sync state. If no specs and no markers exist, it returns "No specs or markers registered." with guidance. Otherwise it prints specs sorted by ID with filepath:line and [deleted] or [unlinked] tags, markers sorted by ID with filepath:line and [deleted] or [unlinked] tags, and links with marker to spec and [synced] or [DRIFTED] status.

When reset is called with a single ID argument, the CLI dispatches to ResetOrphan. If the ID contains a dot, it is treated as a spec ID; otherwise as a marker ID. On success, prints "Removed deleted spec/marker X from drift.pin" with exit code 0.

When link fails because the marker is unknown, the error message explains that markers must be D! id=shortcode comment lines and lists all available markers. When link fails because the spec is unknown, the error message explains that spec IDs are module-qualified and lists all available specs.
